summary: New T13 transcendence command: play-on <device-name> resolves a friendly name against live /me/player/devices and the cached devices_seen table.
reason: Spotify's playback API only takes opaque device IDs; users have to list devices and copy/paste. play-on closes the loop with name-based resolution backed by the local cache, so devices that aren't currently online (Echo speakers Alexa hasn't woken yet) still surface a typed wake-hint instead of a raw 404. 18 unit tests for the name-resolution precedence (exact > prefix > substring) and the device-type-branched wake hint. mcp:read-only annotation deliberately omitted (mutating); pp:typed-exit-codes='0,2' set so ambiguous/no-match (exit 2) counts as verify pass.
